name: W_CIDeconvolve_benchmark
description: 3D/2D deconvolution for widefield and confocal microscopy images with multiple algorithm backends (sdeconv, CI SHB-RL, pycudadecon, deconwolf, DeconvolutionLab2, redlionfish, scikit-image). Supports optional GPU acceleration via CUDA and OpenCL.

inputs.name: Tiling
inputs.description: XY tiling mode for large images. 'none': disable tiling. 'custom': use tile size limits from the Tile limits parameter.

inputs.name: Tile limits
inputs.description: Maximum tile dimensions as 'max_xy, max_z'. Only used when Tiling is set to 'custom'. The image is split into the minimum number of XY tiles so each tile fits within these limits.

inputs.name: Compute device
inputs.description: Force compute device for sdeconv/deconwolf/redlionfish backends. 'auto' uses CUDA if available, otherwise CPU.

inputs.name: Numerical aperture
inputs.description: Override objective numerical aperture. 'auto' uses value from image metadata (default 1.4 if missing).

inputs.name: Immersion medium
inputs.description: Immersion medium refractive index for PSF generation. 'auto' uses value from image metadata (default: oil 1.515).

inputs.name: Sample / mounting medium
inputs.description: Refractive index of the sample or mounting medium for PSF generation. 'auto' uses image metadata if available, otherwise defaults to Vectashield (1.45).

inputs.name: Microscope type
inputs.description: Override microscope type for PSF model. 'auto' uses value from image metadata (default 'widefield' if missing). Confocal uses emission × excitation PSF when excitation wavelengths are provided.

inputs.name: Emission wavelengths
inputs.description: Override emission wavelengths in nm, comma-separated (one per channel). 'auto' uses values from image metadata (default 520 nm if missing). Example: '520, 600'.

inputs.name: Excitation wavelengths
inputs.description: Excitation wavelengths in nm, comma-separated (one per channel). Used for confocal PSF generation (emission × excitation model). 'auto' uses values from image metadata; falls back to squared emission PSF if unavailable. Example: '405, 488'.

inputs.name: Z-projection
inputs.description: Output a 2D Z-projection instead of the full 3D stack. 'mip': maximum intensity projection, 'sum': sum projection (converted to float32 if needed), 'none': keep full 3D stack. Ignored for 2D input images.

inputs.name: Perform Benchmark
inputs.description: If true, run the benchmark suite instead of single deconvolution. The benchmark records performance metrics, writes structured CSV + provenance outputs, and adds quality metrics when ground-truth images are available.

inputs.name: Benchmark iterations
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of iteration counts for benchmarking, e.g. '20, 40, 60'. Only used when Perform Benchmark is enabled.

inputs.name: Benchmark methods
inputs.description: Which set of methods to benchmark. Each tier adds methods to the previous. 'all' includes every public backend. Methods that are unsupported on the current machine are written to the CSV as skipped with a reason.
value-choices: sdeconv_rl, pycudadecon_rl_cuda, ci_rl, ci_rl_tv, deconwolf_rl, deconwolf_shb, redlionfish_rl
sdeconv_wiener, pycudadecon_rl_cuda, ci_rl, ci_rl_tv, sdeconv_rl, skimage_cucim_rl, deconvlab2_landweber

inputs.name: Benchmark crop
inputs.description: If true, centre-crop images to tile-size limits before benchmarking (fast, avoids tiling). If false, process full-size images (may use tiling depending on tiling settings). Only used when Perform Benchmark is enabled.

inputs.name: Benchmark one image
inputs.description: If true, benchmark only the first image (sorted by name) from the input folder. If false, benchmark all input images. Only used when Perform Benchmark is enabled.

inputs.name: Benchmark montage
inputs.description: If true, generate visual montage PNGs in addition to the benchmark CSV and provenance JSON. If false, only structured benchmark outputs are written.

inputs.name: Save console log
inputs.description: If true, save a copy of the console output to a .log file in the output folder.
